# services.postsrsd

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.postsrsd`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.postsrsd.configurePostfix` | `boolean` | Whether to configure the required settings to use postsrsd in the local Postfix instance. |
| `services.postsrsd.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the postsrsd SRS server for Postfix.. |
| `services.postsrsd.group` | `string` | Group for the daemon |
| `services.postsrsd.package` | `package` | The postsrsd package to use. |
| `services.postsrsd.secretsFile` | `absolute path` | Secret keys used for signing and verification. ::: {.note} The secret will be generated, if it does not exist at the given path. ::: |
| `services.postsrsd.settings` | `open submodule of attribute set of (boolean or floating point number or signed integer or absolute path or string or list of string)` | Configuration options for the postsrsd.conf file. See the [example configuration](https://github.com/roehling/postsrsd/blob/2.0.11/doc/postsrsd.conf) for possible values. |
| `services.postsrsd.settings.chroot-dir` | `string` | Path to chroot into at runtime as an additional layer of protection. ::: {.note} We confine the runtime environment through systemd hardening instead, so this option is read-only. ::: |
| `services.postsrsd.settings.domains` | `list of string` | List of local domains, that do not require rewriting. |
| `services.postsrsd.settings.secrets-file` | `string` | Path to the file containing the secret keys. ::: {.note} Secrets are passed using `LoadCredential=` on the systemd unit, so this options is read-only. Configure {option}`services.postsrsd.secretsFile` instead. |
| `services.postsrsd.settings.separator` | `one of "-", "=", "+"` | SRS tag separator used in generated sender addresses. Unless you have a very good reason, you should leave this setting at its default. |
| `services.postsrsd.settings.socketmap` | `string matching the pattern ^(unix\|inet):.+` | Listener configuration in socket map format native to Postfix configuration. |
| `services.postsrsd.settings.srs-domain` | `null or string` | Dedicated mail domain used for ephemeral SRS envelope addresses. Recommended to configure, when hosting multiple unrelated mail domains (e.g. for different customers), to prevent privacy issues. Set to `null` to not configure any `srs-domain`. |
| `services.postsrsd.settings.unprivileged-user` | `string` | Unprivileged user to drop privileges to. ::: {.note} Our systemd unit never runs postsrsd as a privileged process, so this option is read-only. ::: |
| `services.postsrsd.user` | `string` | User for the daemon |
